title: [APIv2] Environment variables are not initialized in container
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug
REST API v2

**Description**
Related to https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/7235

Container created with REST API v2 does not have environment variables set

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run following script
```
# create Dockerfile
echo -e "from alpine\nenv foo=\"bar\"" > /tmp/Dockerfile
podman build -t test /tmp/

# start podman REST API
killall podman
podman --log-level=debug --runtime=/usr/bin/crun system service -t 0 tcp:localhost:8880 2>&1>/tmp/podman-rest.log &

#REST API v2 - create container, show log, inspect container and remove it
curl -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" 'http://127.0.0.1:8880/containers/create?name=test' --data '{"name":"test","Image":"localhost/test:latest", "Cmd":["printenv"]}'
curl -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" 'http://127.0.0.1:8880/containers/test/start'
curl --output - -H "Content-Type: application/json" "http://127.0.0.1:8880/containers/test/logs?stdout=true&stderr=true"
podman logs test
podman inspect test > /tmp/test-test-podman-api.txt
curl -X DELETE -H "Content-Type: application/json" 'http://127.0.0.1:8880/containers/test'
```


**Describe the results you received:**
```
$ podman logs test
PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
TERM=xterm
HOSTNAME=2c2ca1c98053
HOME=/root
container=libpod
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
I expect the log would show the environment variables as when running the same commands using CLI below
Relevant part is line foo=bar which is missing when using REST API v2

```
# CLI
podman create --name test localhost/test:latest printenv
podman start test
podman logs test
podman inspect test > /tmp/test-test-podman.txt
podman rm test
```

```
$ podman logs test
PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
TERM=xterm
container=podman
containers=podman
foo=bar
HOSTNAME=b224b3dc9ab7
HOME=/root
```
```
**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I've tested it on FC31 and FC32. I have not tested with podman-remote

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.1.1
API Version:  2.0.0
Go Version:   go1.13.15
Built:        Wed Sep 30 21:51:15 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.16.1
  cgroupManager: systemd
  cgroupVersion: v2
  conmon:
    package: conmon-2.0.21-2.fc31.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.21, commit: d9f047cb413248e30a1ec560547d9b203396c31b'
  cpus: 8
  distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    version: "31"
  eventLogger: journald
  hostname: localhost.localdomain
  idMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 21528
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 21528
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
  kernel: 5.8.11-100.fc31.x86_64
  linkmode: dynamic
  memFree: 1175146496
  memTotal: 16381120512
  ociRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: crun-0.15-5.fc31.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 0.15
      commit: 56ca95e61639510c7dbd39ff512f80f626404969
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /run/user/21528/podman/podman.sock
  rootless: true
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /bin/slirp4netns
    package: slirp4netns-1.1.4-1.fc31.x86_64
    version: |-
      slirp4netns version 1.1.4
      commit: b66ffa8e262507e37fca689822d23430f3357fe8
      libslirp: 4.1.0
      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 1
  swapFree: 8155656192
  swapTotal: 8252289024
  uptime: 116h 24m 10.29s (Approximately 4.83 days)
registries:
  search:
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org

    number: 5
    paused: 0

    stopped: 5
  graphDriverName: vfs

  runRoot: /run/user/21528/run

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-2.1.1-3.fc31.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Laptop

comments.body: I was unable to recreate on master, I believe it was taken care of in cde367c1c0b348366fcaf4d8fac3b753de8456d5
@rhatdan @mheon Does this commit need to be back ported to other branches?
No I don't think so.  We should just get ready for another release.
